word_list.word: a fool may earn money, but it takes a wise man to keep it
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ fˈuːl mˈeɪ ˈɜːn mˈʌni bˌʌt ɪt tˈeɪks ɐ wˈaɪz mˈæn tə kˈiːp ɪt

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is not enough to simply earn money, but it is important to have the knowledge and skill to manage, preserve, and increase one's wealth over time

word_list.word: a penny saved is a penny earned
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ pˈɛni sˈeɪvd ɪz ɐ pˈɛni ˈɜːnd

senses.definition: used to imply that saving money is just as valuable as earning money, as both contribute to increasing one's overall wealth

word_list.word: better go to bed supperless than (to|) rise in debt
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ ɡˌoʊ tə bˈɛd sˈʌpɚləs ðɐn tʊ ɹˈaɪz ɪn dˈɛt

senses.definition: used to imply that it is preferable to avoid debt by living within one's means, even if it means going without a meal

word_list.word: better give a shilling than lend a half-crown
word_list.phonetic_text: bɛ.tɜr gɪv ə ʃɪ.lɪng ðæn lɛnd ə hæf-kraʊn
word_list.phonetic_am_text: be.tēr giv ē shi.ling dhān lend ē hāf-kraoon
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ ɡˈɪv ɐ ʃˈɪlɪŋ ðɐn lˈɛnd ɐ hˈæfkɹˈaʊn

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is better to give a small sum of money as a gift than to lend a larger sum that may create financial strain and harm the relationship

word_list.word: be just before you are generous
word_list.phonetic_text: bi: ʤʌst bɪ.fɔ:r ju ɑr ʤɛ.nɜ.rəs
word_list.phonetic_am_text: bi jast bi.fawr yoo aar je.nē.rēs
word_list.pronunciation: biː dʒˈʌst bɪfˌoːɹ juː ɑːɹ dʒˈɛnɚɹəs

senses.definition: used to imply that one should prioritize fulfilling their obligations and acting with fairness and integrity before offering help to others
used to imply that it is important to keep track of debts and pay them back promptly, in order to maintain positive and long-lasting relationships

word_list.word: neither a borrower nor a lender be
word_list.phonetic_text: naɪ.ðɜr ə bɑ.roʊɜr nɔ:r ə lɛn.dɜr bi:
word_list.phonetic_am_text: nai.dhēr ē baa.roooēr nawr ē len.dēr bi
word_list.pronunciation: nˈiːðɚɹ ɐ bˈɔːɹoʊɚ nˈɔːɹ ɐ lˈɛndɚ bˈiː

senses.definition: used to advise against borrowing or lending money, as it can lead to financial and interpersonal difficulties

examples.x: When it comes to managing my personal finances, I try to follow the advice of the saying 'neither a borrower nor a lender be' and avoid taking on debt unless it's absolutely necessary.

word_list.word: {not} spend it all in one place
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt spˈɛnd ɪt ˈɔːl ɪn wˈʌn plˈeɪs

senses.definition: used to suggest that one should be cautious with their spending, and not waste all their money in one location or on one thing
used to imply that even though someone may have very little money, they can still find ways to make ends meet and survive

word_list.word: a full cup needs a steady hand
word_list.phonetic_text: ə fʊl kʌp nidz ə stɛ.di hænd
word_list.phonetic_am_text: ē fool kap nidz ē ste.di hānd
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ fˈʊl kˈʌp nˈiːdz ɐ stˈɛdi hˈænd

senses.definition: used to suggest that anything valuable, such as a position of power or material wealth, should be handled with care and responsibility, as even a small mistake can have significant consequences

word_list.word: better to drink the milk than to eat the cow
word_list.phonetic_text: bɛ.tɜr tu: drɪnk ðə mɪlk ðæn tu: i:t ðə kaʊ
word_list.phonetic_am_text: be.tēr too drink dhē milk dhān too it dhē kaoo
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ tə dɹˈɪŋk ðə mˈɪlk ðɐn tʊ ˈiːt ðə kˈaʊ

senses.definition: used to advise that it is more beneficial to take a smaller but sustainable benefit from a resource or opportunity, rather than trying to consume or exhaust it completely for short-term gain

word_list.word: better a steady dime than a rare dollar
word_list.phonetic_text: bɛ.tɜr ə stɛ.di daɪm ðæn ə rɛr dɑ.lər
word_list.phonetic_am_text: be.tēr ē ste.di daim dhān ē rer daa.lēr
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚɹ ɐ stˈɛdi dˈaɪm ðˌænɚ ɹˈɛɹ dˈɑːlɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is preferable to have a steady and dependable source of income or wealth, even if it is modest, rather than relying on occasional large sums that may not be sustainable or reliable
